items.title: Abnormal
items.summary: What's open today? Anything? Everything? I don't know. One of those…
items.content_html:
What's open today?

Anything?

Everything?

I don't know.

One of those odd holidays.

Falls on a Saturday.

Do you get Friday off?

Saturday?

Both?

Let's celebrate this weekend (aka this strong beginning).

Whether it's started or will start tonight.

And thank you to those who work this holiday weekend while we all party.

Thank you for working.

Wake up!

Monday is when everything will go back to normal.

And who wants to be that?

Not me.

The last day of being 249.

Old(ish).

God bless.

items.title: Free For All
items.summary: Where are your highest successes? Behind you? In front? Under? Above?…
items.content_html:
Where are your highest successes?

Behind you?

In front?

Under?

Above?

Can you see them?

Feel them?

Smell them?

Where they at?

How do you measure such things?

How are you keeping score?

When you go, and we all have to go (speaking of, it's kinda getting late), how do you wish to be remembered?

Don't leave an o-bitch-uary for someone else to write.

Alex had a lot of problems with you people. Here are the top 100:

Craft an apex opus.

Not a great work.

Magnum meh.

Your peak art.

Apex!

Your highest.

Opus!

I don't mean a got dang job.

Unless that's your thing.

Do they still give you a pin for 30 years?

I mean something that you can leave behind.

That you will leave behind.

A verse.

📝

Your verse.

When you have to go.

When it's time.

We all gotta.

Speaking of.

Get out.

There's the door:

🚪

Peace.

items.title: You'll Probably Break A Knuckle
items.summary: When you throw a punch, you want to stay firmly grounded.
items.content_html:
When you throw a punch, you want to stay firmly grounded.

Contrary to popular opinion, you don't throw a punch with your fist.

You throw it with your feet.

Ain't that a kick?

From the ground.

From the earth itself.

Start from the core of the planet.

🌏

Work your way up.

Feet.

Calves.

Thighs.

Butt.

Hips.

Torso.

Shoulders.

Then the fist.

Exhale!

🤛

Be quick.

This isn't an elevator at the mall.

Speed is power.

The tendency is to want to clinch your fist immediately.

Right when you start to wind up.

But you shouldn't.

It'll slow you down.

In more ways than one.

Loose like a whip.

Until it's time to snap!

Right before point of impact.

🐍

Aim where you think he'll be.

Not where he is.

If it's a noob, just throw it where he is.

Get it over with.

Over and out.

Jack Dempsey used to say you sort of sit on it.

You drop down as you throw.

Almost in free fall as you swing.

Use gravity as a force multiplier.

Got the whole universe coming behind your fist.

Don't be discouraged and disheartened if you miss.

You will miss.

Discouragement is fatigue.

Disheartenment (real word?) is defeat.

You don't want that when you have to swing hams.

Chin down.

Hands and eyes up.

Relax.

Keep breathing.

From the belly.

Loose.

Then explode!

Again!

Again!!

Again!!!

As a man of peace, this is all for entertainment purposes only.

I don't want you getting hurt.

But it might happen anyway.

✌

items.title: Jericho
items.summary: 🎵 And you ask me what I want this year, And…
items.content_html:
🎵 And you ask me what I want this year,

And I try to make this kind and clear.

Some songs.

Sound like a letter.

Just a chance that maybe we'll find better days.

A love letter.

A how I'm doing letter.

A wisdom letter.

'Cause I don't need boxes wrapped in string*,

And designer love and empty things.

Just a chance that maybe we'll find better days. 🎶

Maybe all of the above.

Maybe, all of the above.

Don't worry. Not an error. He just likes to repeat himself for dramatic effect. And throw in an extra comma or two.

Do people write love letters anymore?

Love texts, maybe.

Maybe.

Love emojis, for sure.

❤️

The letter song I quoted starts with a piano.

Then you hear the thump of a bass drum.

Then a tambourine (I didn't know you spelled it like that).

And I just like it.

One thing.

Then another.

Then one more.

That's a lot of thens.

Layers of thens.

Then (last one), all at once!

Just another musing from an absurd amused fellow.

Thank you.

Thank, you.

🎈

May you find better.

 

 

* - It's "strings" plural in the song but I like the singular "string" better. "[Humming the first part]....Wrapped in string." So inaccuracy and personal quirk wins here. As it often does. Perhaps I have explained too much. If you don't like it, go get your own musings column!

items.title: Call Me AL (Al looks too much like AI)
items.summary: People used to ask me: "What's your name?" When I'd tell…
items.content_html:
People used to ask me:

"What's your name?"

When I'd tell them.

They would sometimes look disappointed.

They would sometimes ask, "No, your REAL name."

Like my name wasn't real.

Which always confused me.

And it took awhile (like some things do) for me to understand what they were asking.

My Korean name.

They were expecting something ethnic.

Like when someone used to ask where I was from.

New Yawk.

They were really asking what my bloodlines were.

Why I looked the way I did.

Growing up, my folks always wanted me to have a Korean name.

I think it's a mandatory thing.

But they never did.

I refused.

I knew my name.

I already had it.

Why another one?

And as I was growing up, over the years, they'd throw out a name.

"How about [insert a single syllable sound three times]"?

Mmm. 

No.

"How about [another three syllable sound]?"

No thank you.

One day, I offered a counter:

"How about [some random name I probably thought was cool on a video game]?"

"No. You can't be that. It's the word for God."

"Oooh, I like that! I want that one!!!"

So, they pretty much left it alone after that.

You can't be named after God.

At least, that's what my folks thought.

So I'm Alex.

Stuck with Alex.

You love your name.

No Korean name.

Because my folks wouldn't name me after God.

I've been called a lot of things.

Never God, but every name under Him.

But I know my name.

If you're one of my people (not racially, but tribally), you can drop the last two letters.

But don't ever call me.

Text.

Or email.

Email might be best.

God bless,

AC

items.title: Gleaming The Cube
items.summary: As an old(ish) man, you'd think I'd know it all. At…
items.content_html:
As an old(ish) man, you'd think I'd know it all.

At least, that's what I thought.

That I'd know it all.

"By the time I reach age [oldish], I'll have it all figured out."

But here I am:

Old(ish).

When did that happen?

And you know what?

I learn something new every day.

That the older I get, the more I realize how much I do not know.

Maybe I just forgot.

I do that more, you know.

It's humbling.

And I suppose I need that.

Sometimes.

Only sometimes.

A gentle reminder.

I think my faith in the world has been renewed today.

Or at least, given a nice polish.

A boost.

World's gleaming.

Just look at it:

🌎

Beautiful.

When you have the chance to work alongside people who bring it, who are it, and who have it, well that's just something special.

Something rare.

But still there.

They are still out there.

Good people.

Good people.

BadMFs.

Let me hear ya!

Something that, as an old(ish) curmudgeon monk living his second chance at life dream, I sometimes forget.

Too much news.

Have faith in humanity.

Trust your spidey sense.

Learn something new.

Remember something you forgot.

Gleam.

⬛

items.title: Gotta Start Somewhere
items.summary: The first law firm I worked for as a freshly minted…
items.content_html:
The first law firm I worked for as a freshly minted attorney had this really cool ampersand in its logo.

I worked at another firm as a law student before that, but their ampersand was just normal.

Great firm, though.

Which is fine.

A normal ampersand is fine.

&

Fine.

The cool ampersand was bold, asymmetric, unique, and golden.

Like me.

I don't remember much else.

Yes you do.

Cool logo, though.

For a law firm.

🔣

That's the whole post?

items.title: Jubilee, or Jubi Lee (Korean Man)
items.summary: I write these musings to clear my brain. It was, for…
items.content_html:
I write these musings to clear my brain.

It was, for quite a long time, perhaps still is, cluttered and messy.

Like an old(ish) attic.

Full of dusty memories.

Long forgotten ideas.

And opinions bottled up to ferment like a good kimchi.

Or booch.

You know, kombucha?

Booch is a fun word to say.

Booch.

Tee hee.

I've gone in this attic many times since I started this thing.

Bringing things out into the daylight.

Cleaning and clearing up a bit.

Releasing them out into the wild.

Freeing them.

It occurs to me that I have started to repeat myself in these rambles.

That I have multiples of things I treasure.

Redundancies cubed.

3 is 1...

As one should.

As four should.

That I hammer home certain points.

Over and over.

Pounding 'em.

When I don't know what I'm going to write about (which happens to be the majority of the time), I tend to ramble.

Then I read it and think, "Hmm, I wrote about this before."

I suppose that's the point of iterating.

Viewpoint.

Refine.

Repeat.

Improve.

I have good days and bad days now.

As we all do.

My bads suck, but they aren't all bad.

There are always golden linings.

Not all bad.

A lot of good.

Just like me.

And hopefully, you, too.

What does this make possible?

Thank you for coming to my attic.

Why are you in my attic?

I hope you find what you're looking for.

I know I have.

items.title: All The Worlds
items.summary: As a fan of old(ish) professional wrestling, you start to see…
items.content_html:
As a fan of old(ish) professional wrestling, you start to see the world as a work.

Work is a term used in PW to describe something that is prearranged.

Some call it fake, but that's not necessarily a bad thing.

Everything is staged.

If not rehearsed, it is at least discussed. 

Prepared for.

Sure there's chaos.

Randomness.

But steps typically lead up to it.

That were talked about.

Practiced.

There's an old joke I heard about a guy who tried to get into Shakespeare.

He read all of Billy's works and just didn't really like it.

Why?

He explained, "It's too full of cliches."

...

Laugh.

Where was I?

Have you ever willed something into existence?

I mean, set a fantastic goal, an almost impossible one, and achieved it?

Things always look so possible after the fact.

Looking back.

You start stacking wins, and the impossible seems not only possible, but probable.

Inevitable.

Fake it till you make it.

Right.

If you fake it and you make it, was it fake?

I'm not sure.

And does fake just mean lying?

Must you lie to "make" it?

Be real.

Tell the truth.

Do they?

Don't hate the players. 

Love the game.

Your move.

And...action!

🎬

items.title: Some Say It Is So
items.summary: Speak it into existence. Go ahead. Say what you have been…
items.content_html:
Speak it into existence.

Go ahead.

Say what you have been aiming for.

I don't mean in your head.

I mean out your mouth.

Lemme hear it.

Too loud.

Whisper it.

Spoken words are powerful.

And sometimes, the calmest words said are the fieriest.

Are the finest.

So speak easy.

Let me hear you.

Are you the one?
